content: The state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory architectures, which are designed to prioritize storing high-value novel experiences for efficient querying, can be repurposed as a built-in intrinsic reward signal for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning. Instead of comparing ICM (Intrinsic Curiosity Module) and RND (Random Network Distillation) reward prediction error as sources of curiosity, an active memory selection mechanism directly ranks experiences by how much they improve the agent’s future learning efficiency to generate curiosity rewards.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
